Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: OR MS QS appears at the start of every case and PK DP D appears at the end with LB sometimes present and sometimes skipped

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: From the repeated traces the core backbone is inferred as OR then MS then QS then assembly related behavior then testing related behavior then PK then optional LB then DP then D

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Cases 1 2 4 and 5 show AS alone or AS IQC AS or AS IQC AS IQC AS and case 3 shows AS IQC IQC AS IQC AS so the assembly segment contains repetition around AS and IQC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: A loop is proposed for the assembly area with AS as the do part and an IQC based redo part so that AS is executed first and further IQC related rework can bring execution back to AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Using the stated loop semantics star of A B means execute A then either exit or execute B and then A again so star of TST RT captures TST RT TST and repeated retesting exactly

Name: Validation - PE

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Modeling one or more IQC with a loop on IQC and a silent transition and then using that as the redo part of the AS loop yields sequences such as AS IQC AS AS IQC IQC AS and AS IQC IQC AS IQC AS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This nested assembly loop replays all listed cases including the simpler path with no IQC the paths with one IQC per rework and the path with consecutive IQC before returning to AS

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: No evidence of concurrency appears in the log because every case respects the same overall precedence and no pair of activities swaps order across cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the StrictPartialOrder can be a simple chain of dependencies OR to MS MS to QS QS to assembly loop assembly loop to testing loop testing loop to PK PK to optional labeling optional labeling to DP and DP to D
